commit.name: Weather Card
commit.description: Data display with props, design tokens, and responsive layout
commit.prompt:
Build a weather card component. It should display:
- Current temperature (large, prominent)
- Weather condition with an appropriate icon/emoji (sunny, cloudy, rainy, etc.)
- Humidity percentage
- Wind speed
- A 5-day forecast strip at the bottom showing day name, icon, and high/low temps

All data must come from props — never hardcode weather data. Use CSS variables from the design system for all colors and spacing.

issues.description: Emojis used for weather icons lack role='img' and aria-labels, making the forecast inaccessible to screen readers.
issues.fix: Add role='img' and an appropriate aria-label to the <div> wrapping the forecast emojis. For the main icon, use aria-hidden='true' since the condition text is already visible.

issues.description: The card and forecast items are completely static.
issues.fix: Add subtle hover states (like a slight background color change or elevation increase) to the forecast days to make the component feel more polished and tactile.

issues.description: Using useMemo for simple default prop merging is slightly over-engineered.
issues.fix: Use standard parameter destructuring with default values (e.g., function Component({ location = '...', ... })).

evaluations.critique: The weather card implements all requirements perfectly with a clean, hierarchical visual design. However, it lacks accessibility attributes for the emoji icons and misses an opportunity to add subtle interactive polish to the forecast elements.

issues.description: Emojis used for weather icons lack role='img' and aria-labels, making the forecast inaccessible to screen readers.
issues.fix: Add role='img' and an appropriate aria-label to the <div> wrapping the forecast emojis. For the main icon, use aria-hidden='true' since the condition text is already visible.

issues.description: The card and forecast items are completely static.
issues.fix: Add subtle hover states (like a slight background color change or elevation increase) to the forecast days to make the component feel more polished and tactile.

issues.description: Using useMemo for simple default prop merging is slightly over-engineered.
issues.fix: Use standard parameter destructuring with default values (e.g., function Component({ location = '...', ... })).

evaluation.critique: The weather card implements all requirements perfectly with a clean, hierarchical visual design. However, it lacks accessibility attributes for the emoji icons and misses an opportunity to add subtle interactive polish to the forecast elements.

commit.name: Interactive Periodic Table
commit.description: CSS Grid periodic table with category coloring, search, and detail panel
commit.prompt:
Build an interactive periodic table of elements.

Layout:
- CSS Grid: 18 columns. Place each element using gridRow and gridColumn from its data.
- Each cell: symbol (large, centered), atomic number (small, top-left)
- Color-code by category using design tokens (e.g., noble-gas → one color, halogen → another)
- Search input at top — filters by name or symbol (case-insensitive)
- Click an element → detail panel below grid shows: name, symbol, atomicNumber, atomicWeight, category
- Legend showing each category with its color

All element data comes from props.elements. Use design system CSS variables.

commit.name: Onboarding Wizard
commit.description: Multi-step onboarding flow with step indicator and validation
commit.prompt:
Build a 3-step onboarding wizard. Steps:

Step 1 - Profile Setup:
- Name input (required)
- Email input (required, validate email format)
- Avatar selection (choose from 4 emoji avatars)

Step 2 - Preferences:
- Role selection (dropdown: Developer, Designer, Manager, Other)
- Notification toggle (email notifications on/off)
- Theme preference (light/dark toggle)

Step 3 - Review & Confirm:
- Summary of all selections from steps 1-2
- "Go Back" button to edit
- "Complete Setup" button to submit

Requirements:
- Step indicator at top showing current step (1/2/3) with progress bar
- Back/Next navigation (Next disabled until required fields valid)
- Smooth transition between steps
- Call props.onComplete with all collected data on final submit
- All initial values from props (for editing existing profile)
- Use design system CSS variables for all styling

initialProfile.description: Pre-filled profile data for editing (all fields optional)

avatarOptions.description: Available avatar emojis to choose from

roleOptions.description: Available role options for the dropdown

complete.label: Complete Setup
complete.description: Called with all profile data when user completes onboarding

issues.description: The component code is abruptly truncated at the end, leaving incomplete props, missing closing tags, and invalid syntax.
issues.fix: Ensure the full component is generated, including the complete step 3 action buttons and all closing JSX/React tags.

commit.name: Weather Card
commit.description: Data display with props, design tokens, and responsive layout
commit.prompt:
Build a weather card component. It should display:
- Current temperature (large, prominent)
- Weather condition with an appropriate icon/emoji (sunny, cloudy, rainy, etc.)
- Humidity percentage
- Wind speed
- A 5-day forecast strip at the bottom showing day name, icon, and high/low temps

All data must come from props — never hardcode weather data. Use CSS variables from the design system for all colors and spacing.

issues.description: The forecast section lacks responsive handling or horizontal scrolling, which could lead to overflow issues on narrow screens.
issues.fix: Add a wrapper with overflow-x auto to the forecast container for mobile compatibility.

issues.description: Weather icons are currently just raw strings (emojis/text) without descriptive alt text or aria-labels for screen readers.
issues.fix: Use aria-label attributes or descriptive text spans that are hidden visually but accessible to screen readers to describe the weather condition.

issues.description: The card is purely static; adding a subtle hover scale or shadow lift would improve perceived quality.
issues.fix: Add CSS transition properties for hover states to elevate the interactive feel.

evaluations.critique: The component is functionally complete and follows the design system primitives well. It is clean and well-structured, but requires minor accessibility enhancements for icons and improved handling for smaller viewports to be truly production-grade.

commit.name: Interactive Periodic Table
commit.description: CSS Grid periodic table with category coloring, search, and detail panel
commit.prompt:
Build an interactive periodic table of elements.

Layout:
- CSS Grid: 18 columns. Place each element using gridRow and gridColumn from its data.
- Each cell: symbol (large, centered), atomic number (small, top-left)
- Color-code by category using design tokens (e.g., noble-gas → one color, halogen → another)
- Search input at top — filters by name or symbol (case-insensitive)
- Click an element → detail panel below grid shows: name, symbol, atomicNumber, atomicWeight, category
- Legend showing each category with its color

All element data comes from props.elements. Use design system CSS variables.

issues.description: The card contents (atomic number and symbol) lack proper alignment and spacing, resulting in cramped text.
issues.fix: Use a Flexbox layout within the Card component to align the atomic number to the top-left and center the symbol.

issues.description: The table cells are div elements with onClick handlers but no keyboard support (onKeyDown) or role='button'.
issues.fix: Add role='button', tabIndex={0}, and an onKeyDown handler to the grid cells to ensure keyboard accessibility.

issues.description: Lack of hover states or active transitions on the grid elements makes them feel static.
issues.fix: Add a CSS transition property and a hover effect (e.g., opacity or scale) to the grid cells.

issues.description: The visual hierarchy of the detail panel is quite flat.
issues.fix: Use descriptive labels or a definition list structure for the element details instead of plain Text components.

evaluations.critique: The component successfully implements the complex grid layout requirement and basic functionality. However, it requires more attention to interactive accessibility patterns (keyboard focus) and visual refinement to ensure the elements look polished rather than just functional.

commit.name: Onboarding Wizard
commit.description: Multi-step onboarding flow with step indicator and validation
commit.prompt:
Build a 3-step onboarding wizard. Steps:

Step 1 - Profile Setup:
- Name input (required)
- Email input (required, validate email format)
- Avatar selection (choose from 4 emoji avatars)

Step 2 - Preferences:
- Role selection (dropdown: Developer, Designer, Manager, Other)
- Notification toggle (email notifications on/off)
- Theme preference (light/dark toggle)

Step 3 - Review & Confirm:
- Summary of all selections from steps 1-2
- "Go Back" button to edit
- "Complete Setup" button to submit

Requirements:
- Step indicator at top showing current step (1/2/3) with progress bar
- Back/Next navigation (Next disabled until required fields valid)
- Smooth transition between steps
- Call props.onComplete with all collected data on final submit
- All initial values from props (for editing existing profile)
- Use design system CSS variables for all styling

initialProfile.description: Pre-filled profile data for editing (all fields optional)

avatarOptions.description: Available avatar emojis to choose from

roleOptions.description: Available role options for the dropdown

complete.label: Complete Setup
complete.description: Called with all profile data when user completes onboarding

issues.description: The review step is missing several fields collected during the wizard (Avatar, Notifications, and Theme).
issues.fix: Add the remaining state fields to the summary view in step 3.

issues.description: Missing semantic labels for the step indicator and lack of aria-live regions for state transitions.
issues.fix: Add aria-labels or aria-live='polite' to the container switching between steps to notify screen readers of content changes.

issues.description: Step transition is immediate; adding a slight animation or visual feedback would improve the UX.
issues.fix: Use a simple transition wrapper or CSS animation class on the step container.

evaluations.critique: The component is well-structured and functional, correctly handling validation and state management. To reach production quality, the summary screen needs to display all collected data, and basic ARIA attributes should be added to improve accessibility during step changes.
